resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The public sample rows are preserved as observed output structure only: Report limitation: Heritable component only; Cardiovascular Diseases: Cardiovascular category risk table; Autoimmune Diseases: Autoimmune category risk table. They are not local-user PRS score, ASCVD risk, diabetes risk, obesity risk, lipid status, blood-pressure status, lab finding, medication need, screening need, prevention plan, risk reclassification, ancestry-insensitive score, all-clear reassurance, or actionability findings.

prsModelContext.plainEnglishMeaning: Cardiometabolic Health PRS is reported as an evidence-availability boundary. The local fixture can preserve source-bound sibling sample structure and limitations, but it cannot calculate or interpret a personal cardiometabolic PRS.

prsModelContext.professionalReview: Use validated score provenance, calibration, ancestry applicability, cardiometabolic labs, blood pressure, family history, medication context, and qualified professional review for cardiometabolic decisions.

sampleRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample states that 'Your Risk' is only for the heritable component of the disease, and that environmental, lifestyle, and other non-heritable factors also affect overall susceptibility risk.
sampleRows.description: The sample uses lung cancer as an example, saying lung cancer is only 10% heritable and most cases are associated with lifestyle factors such as smoking.
sampleRows.sourceLabel: Sibling Disease Risk Genetic Test Report sample report
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Extracted from the public Sequencing.com Disease Risk Genetic Test Report sample PDF; used here as sibling output-structure evidence for Cardiometabolic Health PRS, not as a direct Cardiometabolic Health PRS sample report or local cardiometabolic PRS output structure, PRS score, ASCVD risk, diabetes risk, obesity risk, lipid or blood-pressure inference, lab inference, medication need, screening need, prevention plan, risk reclassification, ancestry-insensitive score, low-risk reassurance, or actionability guidance.

sampleRows.geneticAnalysis: Visible sample rows include Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Reduced; Atherosclerosis Typical; Atrial Fibrillation Typical; Cardiovascular Disease Early Onset Very Reduced; COPD Typical; Coronary Artery Disease Typical; Intracranial Aneurysm Elevated; Mitral Valve Prolapse Typical; Myocardial Infarction Reduced; Stroke Elevated; Venous Thromboembolism Typical.
sampleRows.description: The page explains that CAD has a genetic susceptibility component and names KL, NOS3, HSPD1, and CHD13 as example genes used across hundreds of variants.
sampleRows.sourceLabel: Sibling Disease Risk Genetic Test Report sample report
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Extracted from the public Sequencing.com Disease Risk Genetic Test Report sample PDF; used here as sibling output-structure evidence for Cardiometabolic Health PRS, not as a direct Cardiometabolic Health PRS sample report or local cardiometabolic PRS output structure, PRS score, ASCVD risk, diabetes risk, obesity risk, lipid or blood-pressure inference, lab inference, medication need, screening need, prevention plan, risk reclassification, ancestry-insensitive score, low-risk reassurance, or actionability guidance.

sampleRows.geneticAnalysis: Visible sample rows include Allergy/Hay Fever/Eczema Elevated; Ankylosing Spondylitis Typical; Asthma Reduced; Childhood-Onset Asthma Typical; Autoimmune Diseases Typical; Celiac Disease Reduced; Crohn's Disease Typical; Type-1 Diabetes Unlikely; Type-2 Diabetes Typical; Graves' Disease Typical; IBD Reduced; Multiple Sclerosis Very Reduced; Psoriasis Reduced; Rheumatoid Arthritis Very Reduced; Sarcoidosis Reduced; Lupus Reduced; Ulcerative Colitis Reduced.
sampleRows.description: The page explains Type-2 Diabetes susceptibility and names PPARG, KCNJ11, and TCF7L2 as example diabetes-related genes.
sampleRows.sourceLabel: Sibling Disease Risk Genetic Test Report sample report
